name: Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve: loop from Denia
name_original: Parque natural de la marjal de Pego - Oliva — Circular desde Denia
description:
Water is the star of this easy circular route from Dénia train station. To get out and back to the city, you can take advantage of the charming greenway. This runs through an area of agricultural plots where palm trees and fruit trees are greening up. Until the only climb on the route, you pass through a series of small towns that are ideal for taking a break and enjoying some tapas.

The route takes you along the Girona River plain to the foot of the short path, which has a challenging climb up the Pego mountain. During the descent, you enjoy magnificent views of the marsh and the rural landscape of Alicante. Once down, you go along paths between citrus plantations until you reach the Vedat River, a charming course of crystal-clear waters where you can probably get a little wet. From here, you cross the Marjal de Pego - Oliva Natural Park, one of the most important marshes in Alicante, as it is home to a great variety of local birds and flora.

Before returning to the Denia railway, you have a long stretch along a bike path very close to the coast, where you find numerous passages that invite you to take a nice dip in the Mediterranean before finishing the day.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Dénia offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal paths with mountainous terrain and tranquil wetlands. The region is characterized by the imposing Montgó Natural Park, which provides challenging ascents and panoramic views. Riders can explore a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, from old railway lines to dirt tracks through orange groves and vineyards. This varied topography makes Dénia suitable for a wide range of gravel biking experiences. Best gravel bike trails around Dénia The most popular gravel bike trail is Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve: loop from Denia, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through serene wetlands and agricultural fields. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Granadella Cove – View of Cala Granadella loop from Xàbia / Jávea, a difficult 17.9 miles (28.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and offers views of the rugged coastline and coves. Local gravel bikers also love the View of the Valley and the Sea – Moraira Beach loop from Everest Spice, a 35.4 miles (57.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with coastal and valley vistas, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Gravel biking around Dénia is defined by coastal paths, mountainous ascents, and tranquil marshlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides through orange groves to challenging climbs in the Montgó Natural Park. The routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Dénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Dénia?
A: Dénia offers a diverse mix of terrain for gravel biking. You'll find coastal paths, mountainous ascents, and tranquil marshlands. Routes can range from flat, accessible paths through orange groves and wetlands, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922611605/pego-oliva-marsh-nature-reserve-loop-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve loop&lt;/a&gt;, to challenging climbs in the Montgó Natural Park and routes with significant elevation gain like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31027655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley – Vall d'Ebo Pass loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia has several easier options. The Dénia to El Verger section of the Greenway (Vía Verde) is flat and passes through scenic orange groves and fields, making it ideal for a relaxed ride. The Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve also offers flat and accessible routes through wetlands, perfect for those new to gravel biking or looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are the best times of year for gravel biking in Dénia?
A: The warm climate in Dénia allows for year-round cycling. However, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for gravel biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This ensures comfortable riding conditions for exploring both coastal and inland routes.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike routes around Dénia are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922611605/pego-oliva-marsh-nature-reserve-loop-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve: loop from Denia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38194648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Valley and the Sea – Moraira Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and coastal vistas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: Dénia's gravel trails offer access to stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore the imposing Montgó Natural Park with its panoramic views, the picturesque coastline with its beaches and coves, and the tranquil Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve. Along some routes, you might encounter highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Prim&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/964080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgos Riverbed&lt;/a&gt;, or even historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging gravel bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Dénia caters to advanced riders with several difficult routes featuring significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23746741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granadella Cove – View of Cala Granadella loop from Xàbia / Jávea&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging path with impressive coastal views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31027655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley – Vall d'Ebo Pass loop from Oliva&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about Dénia's trails?
A: The komoot community highly rates Dénia's gravel biking experience, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from serene wetlands to challenging mountain paths, and the stunning coastal and valley vistas. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Dénia?
A: For families, the flat and accessible sections of the Dénia to El Verger Greenway are an excellent choice, offering a safe and enjoyable ride through agricultural landscapes. The Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve also provides gentle, flat routes suitable for families looking for a relaxed outing amidst nature.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in the Dénia area?
A: There are over 20 gravel bike trails available around Dénia, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels from beginners to advanced riders.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes may offer views of or pass near historical sites. For instance, you might find trails that provide a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8111238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Dénia Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6680697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church-Fortress of Saint Michael the Archangel&lt;/a&gt; in Murla, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the gravel bike routes?
A: The gravel bike routes around Dénia offer spectacular and varied views. You can expect panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, especially from higher points in the Montgó Natural Park or along coastal routes. Inland, you'll see rolling green hills, vineyards, orange groves, and the tranquil landscapes of the Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Railway Viaduct loop&lt;/a&gt; also offer unique architectural sights.
tips:
A very beautiful route with well-maintained paths, a pleasant, flat stage with no difficulty, highly recommended! The old railway line between Dénia and Gandia, once part of the historic Carcaixent–Dénia line, is now partially accessible as the Vía Verde (Greenway). The section from Dénia to El Verger runs approximately six kilometers in a straight line through orange groves, fields, and over small streams. The route is completely flat and therefore ideal for cycling. The expansive views are particularly charming: on the one hand, the mountains of the hinterland, on the other, the nearby dunes and the Mediterranean Sea.

Those who continue north can reach Oliva and Gandia via the Marjal de Pego-Oliva Nature Park and small country roads. There, rice fields, wetlands with herons, and canals alternate, as well as places like the Muntanyeta Verda and the Font Salada spring. The result is a scenically diverse cycle tour of around forty kilometers, connecting the coast, nature park and agricultural landscape – from the port city of Dénia to historic Gandia.
A nice quiet climb with new road infrastructure. The descent on the north side starts on a smaller road, so beware in the hairpin corners.
This is a beautiful bend in the river with clear waters, perfect for getting a little wet, always keeping in mind the respect for nature.
This marsh is a refuge for aquatic flora and fauna. It is also an important area for growing rice and other fruit trees, especially citrus.

At this point there is a footbridge to cross the Molinell River just before it flows into the sea. The high step allows you to better see the union of the river and sea landscapes. Therefore it is also an ideal point to access the beach.
